emails.subject: Bill Jones Roofing — missed calls after storms
emails.body:
Hey Bill,

Noticed Bill Jones Repair and Reroofs has great reviews on Google — customers clearly trust your work.

Quick question: when someone calls about a roof estimate at 6pm or after a storm and you're on a job — does that call get followed up with the same day?

I build AI phone/text systems for home service businesses in Naples that handle those calls automatically — responds within 2 minutes, qualifies the job, sends you the details.

If it's something you'd want to try, I build and run the whole thing free for 2 weeks so you can see exactly how many calls it catches before you decide if it's worth paying for.

Worth a 15-minute call?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Post-storm surge calls going to voicemail — Naples homeowners call multiple roofers and hire whoever responds first
emails.hook: Storm season urgency + competitive market; roofers win jobs on response time not quality alone
emails.research_notes: Owner/President = Bill, residential/commercial roofing, strong Google reviews, Naples coverage

emails.subject: Advanced Power Electrical — response time question
emails.body:
Hey Luis,

Came across Advanced Power Electrical while researching Naples commercial contractors — impressive project portfolio.

One thing I keep hearing from electrical contractors: it's not the quality of work they lose bids on, it's response time. Client calls at 7am about an emergency job and goes with whoever calls back first.

I build AI systems that handle first-response for service calls — qualifies the job and notifies you within minutes so you're always first to respond, even when you're on-site.

I build and run the full system free for 2 weeks so you can see what it would have caught before committing to anything.

Would a 15-minute call make sense?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Emergency commercial electrical calls going to whoever responds first — being on-site means missed call = lost bid
emails.hook: Commercial electrical = large jobs decided in minutes; response speed beats price most of the time
emails.research_notes: President/Owner = Luis, commercial electrical, strong credentials, Naples + SWFL coverage

emails.subject: Horizon Pool Service — new customer inquiry response
emails.body:
Hey Jeremy,

Horizon Pool Service has solid coverage across Naples — you clearly know the market.

Here's what I keep seeing with pool companies: a homeowner calls 3 services for a quote. First one to respond with a specific answer wins 80% of the time. The other two get "we already found someone, thanks."

I build AI response systems for pool companies that handle incoming calls and texts — qualifies the job, sends pricing info, books the site visit automatically.

I build and run it free for 2 weeks so you can see the actual leads it captures before you decide if it's worth paying for.

15 minutes to see if it fits what you're doing?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: Pool service quote requests go to multiple vendors — first to respond with a real answer wins the job
emails.hook: Naples has high-density pool homes; new customer acquisition is competitive and speed-driven
emails.research_notes: Owner/Operator = Jeremy, pool service + supply, Naples market, recurring service model

emails.subject: Dan House Electric — after-hours call question
emails.body:
Hey Dan,

Dan House Electric has great reviews around Naples — consistent 5-star feedback, which is rare in residential electrical.

One thing I'm curious about: when someone has an electrical issue at night or over the weekend and calls you — what's your process for making sure they don't end up hiring someone else before Monday?

I build AI systems for home service businesses that handle those off-hours calls, qualify the urgency, and reach back out within minutes.

I build and run the whole thing free for 2 weeks so you can see exactly what you're currently missing before deciding whether it's worth paying for.

Worth a quick chat?

William Marceau
Marceau Solutions
wmarceau@marceausolutions.com
emails.pain_point: After-hours electrical calls are urgent — homeowner won't wait until Monday and will hire whoever responds
emails.hook: Residential electrical emergencies (no power, panel issues) force same-day decisions
emails.research_notes: Owner = Dan House, residential electrical, 5-star reviews, Naples coverage
